title: the count() return null while expect 0
poc: create table t1( a int , b int ) ;

select ( select count( * )+1 
          from t1 as tt3 
          where tt3.b < tt2.b )
 from t1 as tt2 ;

select ( select count( * )+1 
 more>         from t1 as tt3 
 more>         where tt3.b < tt2.b )
 more>from t1 as tt2 ;
key_bug_features: count() returns null instead of zero
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE t2 ( id INT PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(50), value FLOAT, created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP );
CREATE TABLE t3 ( ref_id INT REFERENCES t2(id), description TEXT NOT NULL );
SELECT (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM t3 WHERE ref_id IS NULL) + (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM t2 WHERE value IS NULL);
